It is a reusable execution standard, not a plan for one feature or audit. Root `AGENTS.md` supplies the governing behavior, requires this file at the thresholds below, and remains the automatically discovered repository instruction source. Current user instructions and governing product documents continue to define scope and desired results.\n\nRoot `PLANS.md` does not replace `Development_Docs`. When a task requires a durable authored plan or implementation record, store that task-specific artifact under `Development_Docs` in accordance with its rules. A plan may otherwise remain in the task's coordination state or in the user-requested artifact.\n\n## Elapsed-time planning priority\n\nCorrectness, completeness, user intent, safety, required evidence, ownership discipline, and validation quality are hard constraints. Within those constraints, every plan must organize scheduling, delegation, worker capability, integration, and validation to produce the correct integrated result in the minimum practical elapsed time. Speed is an explicit planning objective, not merely a possible by-product of parallelization.\n\nWhen multiple safe and correct decompositions exist, prefer the one expected to finish sooner while delivering the required depth, coverage, and verification. Minimize avoidable waiting, duplicate work, oversized planning ceremony, repeated discovery, delayed review, and unnecessary global validation. Do not trade required evidence, accessibility, security, performance analysis, testing, or acceptance criteria for speed, and do not invent duration estimates or improvement percentages when no timing data was recorded.\n\n## Progressive planning tiers\n\nEvery task gets a parallelization assessment, but planning ceremony scales with task size and risk.\n\n### Tier 0: localized task\n\nUse Tier 0 only when the task is genuinely small, scope is narrow, one file or a tightly related file group is involved, no meaningful independent workstream exists, no shared runtime/database/migration/lockfile/deployment risk exists, and delegation would cost more than it saves.\n\nTier 0 records only the objective, affected scope, quick parallelization assessment, focused validation, and finalization. A distinct verification lane may still be useful.\n\n### Tier 1: standard multi-step task\n\nUse Tier 1 for medium work. Record the objective; scope and exclusions; governing sources; repository/working-tree reality; assumptions and unknowns; compact dependency graph and parallelization assessment; critical path and elapsed-time strategy; lane matrix; subagent communication plan; file and shared-resource ownership; focused testing; integration; and final verification. For each proposed lane, record its expected capacity, quality or coverage, elapsed-time, independence, and coordinator-integration value as defined below. The lane matrix must include the user-visible worker identity, announcement, ownership, lifecycle, deliverable, benefit, time-aware, and integration fields defined below. Use two to five workers when that many meaningful lanes exist.\n\n### Tier 2: large or high-risk task\n\nUse the complete template in this file for repository-wide audits, large implementations, major refactors, multiple feature surfaces, database changes, migrations, deployment, high-risk security work, architecture changes, long-running or cross-computer work, tasks with six or more meaningful lanes, or work with substantial context/coordination-loss risk. Record the critical path and complete elapsed-time strategy, plus the expected capacity, quality or coverage, elapsed-time, independence, and coordinator-integration value of every proposed lane. The complete lane matrix and subagent communication plan are mandatory whenever workers are used. Target six to twelve direct workers when the dependency graph and installed capacity support that many real lanes.\n\n## Progressive planning rule\n\nMinimum safe preflight comes first. Identify immediately ready independent lanes, publish the required compact worker manifest, launch safe read-only lanes without unnecessary delay, and refine the dependency graph and detailed plan while they run. No write lane begins before its requirements, prerequisites, file/resource ownership, and integration boundaries are sufficiently established and every assigned path has passed the write-lane path-verification gate below. Launch write lanes as soon as those conditions are met, keep the highest-value ready work in available slots, and start newly unblocked work immediately. Safe read-only discovery continues while unresolved paths are being identified or verified.\n\nBefore or immediately after any launch, disclose the worker manifest in chat as required by the subagent communication plan; planning refinement never makes a launched worker invisible. Communication must not become a blocking administrative process: do not delay a safe worker merely to produce elaborate formatting. Plans are living coordination artifacts, not paperwork gates; make them detailed enough to protect quality without delaying safe useful work. \"Complete every planning table before starting any worker\" is prohibited when ready read-only lanes exist.\n\n## Planning principles\n\nAn execution plan must:\n\n- be self-contained enough for another coordinator to continue without rediscovering the task;\n- preserve the exact user goal, terminology, constraints, acceptance criteria, methodology, and output format;\n- separate requirements and invariants from implementation choices;\n- identify verified facts, assumptions, unknowns, and canonical sources;\n- represent work as a dependency graph rather than a falsely linear checklist;\n- identify the critical path and organize work to shorten it without weakening any hard constraint;\n- maximize safe, useful concurrency while identifying conflict-prone and serialized work;\n- establish one coordinator, clear lane ownership, and one writer per file at a time;\n- verify assigned write-lane paths against the current repository and distinguish verified existing files, intentional new files, and unresolved read-only paths;\n- identify shared runtime and expensive resources with authoritative owners;\n- define evidence, focused validation, integration validation, and completion criteria before implementation;\n- remain updateable as discoveries change scope, dependencies, ownership, risk, or validation;\n- make every subagent launch and material lifecycle change visible to the user through concise structured chat updates;\n- use completed results promptly and integrate safe independent work incrementally rather than waiting for unrelated lanes;\n- record significant decisions and discoveries without exposing private chain-of-thought; and\n- preserve completed work when the plan changes.\n\n## Capacity and wave scheduling\n\nRecord:\n\n```text\nConfigured open-thread capacity:\nCoordinator reservation:\nMaximum normal direct workers:\nCurrent ready lanes:\nCurrently active lanes:\nBlocked lanes:\nAvailable slots:\nNext ready lanes:\nHighest-value ready-lane queue:\nExclusive resources currently held:\n```\n\nKeep safe capacity filled with the highest-value ready meaningful work, reserve the coordinator when the client counts the root session, and maintain ready and next-ready queues for substantial tasks. Fill each newly available slot immediately rather than waiting for an entire wave. Capacity may remain intentionally unused only when no useful independent work exists or a concrete conflict, dependency, safety, or resource constraint prevents more work. When substantial ready work exists but a slot remains unused, record the reason briefly.\n\nDo not require a fixed worker count when fewer meaningful lanes exist, and do not create a lane merely because capacity is available. When enough real non-overlapping work exists, use the full safe and useful lane count. Avoid idle or artificial workers, stop or collapse redundant work after sufficient evidence exists, preserve results between waves, and keep completed high-priority output moving into review and integration.\n\n## Worker capability and reasoning-speed planning\n\nRoute work for both reliability and elapsed time. Use the fastest capable available worker configuration for mechanical inventories, repository searches, classifications, repetitive comparisons, and bounded read-only scans. Reserve high reasoning effort for work that actually needs it, and use stronger workers for difficult architecture, diagnosis, implementation, reconciliation, and verification. Do not assign maximum reasoning effort to every worker by habit.\n\nRecord any capability or reasoning requirement that materially affects lane success or urgency. Reassign work from an unnecessarily slow configuration when a faster capable worker can complete it reliably, and split broad work when that shortens the critical path without losing useful context. Do not hardcode model names or capabilities that were not verified in the installed client.\n\n## Lane-decomposition quality check\n\nBefore spawning a worker, confirm:\n\n- the lane has a distinct result and evidence or ownership boundary;\n- it can proceed without another lane's unfinished result;\n- it can run without conflicting writes;\n- any exclusive resource and its owner are explicit;\n- its output materially helps the final result and does not duplicate another lane;\n- the selected worker is capable of completing it independently;\n- the return format is clear; and\n- the coordinator can integrate the result.\n\nMerge or serialize lanes that fail this check.\n\nApply this lane-value check to every proposed Tier 1 and Tier 2 lane:\n\n- Does this lane increase useful work capacity?\n- Does it add distinct expertise, evidence, implementation, testing, or verification?\n- Can it reduce the task's critical-path duration?\n- Will this lane reduce total elapsed completion time?\n- Does it remove work from the critical path?\n- Can it execute simultaneously with existing lanes?\n- Will its expected time savings exceed briefing, monitoring, and integration overhead?\n- Would combining it with another lane be faster?\n- Would splitting a slow or broad lane shorten the critical path?\n- Is the worker capability appropriate for the lane's urgency and complexity?\n- Can the result be reviewed and integrated promptly?\n- Is its expected value greater than its delegation and integration overhead?\n- Can its output be integrated without creating conflicts or duplicated work?\n\nA lane should normally be created when it provides at least one strong benefit in speed, capacity, quality, coverage, verification, or risk reduction and does not create disproportionate conflict or coordination cost. A lane that fails this value check should be revised, combined, split, reassigned, serialized, or omitted rather than used ceremonially.\n\n## Write-lane path-verification gate\n\nBefore launching any write-enabled subagent, the coordinator must:\n\n- resolve the current repository root and verify every assigned existing file path against that current repository;\n- record each assigned file using its exact canonical repository path, not shorthand, a basename alone, an inferred filename, or an unverified location;\n- determine whether every assigned path that does not exist is intentionally being created or remains unresolved;\n- label each intended new file explicitly as **intentional new-file ownership** before granting write authorization;\n- label each confirmed existing file explicitly as **verified existing-file ownership**;\n- label every unresolved or unverified path explicitly as **unresolved path â€” read-only**, and keep the affected lane read-only until verification or intentional-new-file classification is complete; and\n- confirm one-writer ownership and integration boundaries only after these path states are known.\n\nA directory or path pattern may define the outer ownership boundary, but it does not replace verification of each assigned existing file. A writer must not launch against an unresolved, inferred, or unverified path. Path resolution must not stall independent work: continue safe read-only discovery while paths are being resolved, then update the plan and visible manifest before changing the lane to write-enabled.\n\n## Complete Tier 2 execution-plan template\n\nTier 2 uses every section below. Tier 1 uses the identified compact subset; Tier 0 uses its stated minimum. \"Not applicable\" is acceptable only with a short reason.\n\n### 1. Task identity\n\n```text\nTask title:\nDate:\nCoordinator:\nRepository and root:\nCurrent branch/worktree and commit:\nGoverning documents:\nRelated issue, phase, audit, or feature:\nCurrent task instructions:\nPlan location/status:\n```\n\nCopy or faithfully summarize the controlling task instructions. Link to the full source when a summary could lose detail.\n\n### 2. Objective\n\nRecord:\n\n- desired end state;\n- user-visible outcome;\n- technical outcome; and\n- why the work is being done.\n\nUse outcome language. Do not describe activity alone as success.\n\n### 3. Scope\n\nRecord:\n\n- included work;\n- explicit exclusions;\n- areas and behaviors that must remain unchanged;\n- existing completed or uncommitted work that must be preserved; and\n- actions that require separate user authority.\n\nParallelization never broadens this scope.\n\n### 4. Sources of truth\n\nList, in priority order:\n\n- current user instructions;\n- governing specifications and approved `Development_Docs` material;\n- maintained architecture, security, testing, and operational documentation;\n- canonical code and data directories;\n- relevant tests and runtime evidence;\n- external dependencies or authoritative external documentation; and\n- the rule to apply when sources disagree.\n\nState which facts were verified in the current task and which were inherited or require confirmation.\n\n### 5. Repository reality check\n\nBefore delegation or modification, capture:\n\n```text\nGit repository root:\nCanonical remote/repository:\nActive application:\nActive branch and HEAD:\nUpstream and ahead/behind/diverged state:\nComplete working-tree classification:\nCanonical startup command:\nRequired application port:\nRelevant focused and full validation commands:\nGenerated, archived, obsolete, or excluded trees:\nExisting uncommitted work and owner/provenance if known:\nSafe synchronization action taken or deferred:\n```\n\nFor this repository, verify rather than assume the current baseline. The expected canonical shape is one root Next.js application on `main`, normal startup through `npm run dev:full`, and normal development port `3000`. If reality differs, stop treating the expectation as fact and update the plan.\n\nDo not inspect secrets, overwrite local databases, or treat generated validation copies as source. Classify unrelated and pre-existing work before editing.\n\n### 6. Assumptions and unknowns\n\nUse this table:\n\n| ID  | Assumption or unknown | Impact if wrong | Verification method | Responsible lane | Can work proceed first? | Status/evidence |\n| --- | --------------------- | --------------- | ------------------- | ---------------- | ----------------------- | --------------- |\n| A1  |                       |                 |                     |                  | Yes/No                  |                 |\n\nDo not silently convert an unknown into a requirement or conclusion.\n\n### 7. Parallelization assessment\n\nAnswer explicitly:\n\n- Which w…24 tokens truncated…t plan;\r\n* phase reports.\r\n\r\n## Step 11: Final integration\r\n\r\nRun supported repository gates under one validation owner.\r\n\r\n## Step 12: Synchronization and stop\r\n\r\n* synchronize Development_Docs;\r\n* synchronize Codex chat archive;\r\n* validate synchronization;\r\n* stop for review;\r\n* do not begin Phase 3 automatically.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Parallelization Rules\r\n\r\nParallelize aggressively only after shared contracts are frozen.\r\n\r\n## Safe parallel lanes\r\n\r\n### Lane A\r\n\r\n```text\r\nVoyage Chart\r\nShipâ€™s Log\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane B\r\n\r\n```text\r\nTreasure Altar\r\nArtifact Inspection\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane C\r\n\r\n```text\r\nCompanion Header\r\nCompanion Navigation\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane D\r\n\r\n```text\r\nQuartermaster\r\nAccessGate\r\nlogin final-state handoff\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane E\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPageFlip source/clone boundary\r\nPageFlip tests\r\n```\r\n\r\n### Lane F\r\n\r\nRead-only requirement reconciliation and documentation verification.\r\n\r\n## Single-owner files\r\n\r\nOnly one owner may edit:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nsrc/animation/core/animation-types.ts\r\nsrc/animation/core/ownership.ts\r\nsrc/animation/director/AnimationDirector.ts\r\nsrc/animation/director/scene-registry.ts\r\nsrc/animation/scenes/scene-utils.ts\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnly one owner at a time may edit:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPlayerExperience.tsx\r\nPageFlipBook.tsx\r\nQuartermaster.tsx\r\nAnimationProvider.tsx\r\nglobal animation styles\r\npackage.json\r\nlockfile\r\nPrisma schema\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnly one owner controls:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nport 3000\r\nvalidation ports\r\ntest server\r\nbrowser automation\r\ndatabase\r\nseed\r\nmigrations\r\nfull E2E\r\nproduction build\r\nnpm run validate\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo parallel lane may invent a competing SceneHost or ownership API.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Required Unit Tests\r\n\r\n## 26.1 SceneHost tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n1. unique host registration;\r\n2. duplicate host rejection;\r\n3. unique scene-instance identity;\r\n4. host-local query;\r\n5. identical part names across hosts;\r\n6. stale instance rejection;\r\n7. detached host cleanup;\r\n8. nested host behavior;\r\n9. external target handles;\r\n10. outside-host rejection;\r\n11. unmount during scene;\r\n12. cleanup idempotency;\r\n13. simultaneous Player and Quartermaster hosts;\r\n14. development-showcase isolation.\r\n\r\nPass condition:\r\n\r\n* no scene resolves another hostâ€™s target;\r\n* cleanup removes registrations;\r\n* stale handles fail.\r\n\r\n## 26.2 Ownership tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nGSAP vs Motion\r\nGSAP vs CSS\r\nMotion vs CSS\r\nMotion vs PageFlip\r\nGSAP vs PageFlip\r\nGSAP vs Rive container\r\nGSAP vs Lottie container\r\nMotion layoutId vs GSAP transform\r\ndnd-kit vs Motion transform\r\ndnd-kit vs GSAP transform\r\n```\r\n\r\nCases:\r\n\r\n1. first claim granted;\r\n2. compatible same-runtime claim;\r\n3. conflict rejected;\r\n4. rejected runtime cannot write;\r\n5. release permits later claim;\r\n6. cleanup releases claims;\r\n7. property-group conflict;\r\n8. non-overlapping properties allowed;\r\n9. stale claim removed;\r\n10. interruption releases claim;\r\n11. fallback releases claim;\r\n12. nested wrapper claims work.\r\n\r\n## 26.3 Target-integrity fixtures\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncorrect host target\r\nanother host with same part name\r\nhidden source\r\nvisible clone\r\nstale clone\r\npermanent section target\r\ntemporary event target\r\ndetached target\r\nzero-box target\r\ntransparent target\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun high-risk part-name tests.\r\n\r\nOnly the declared host and instance may qualify.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Required Component Tests\r\n\r\n## Voyage Chart\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* Motion owns outer layout;\r\n* GSAP owns inner marker visual;\r\n* no same-node transform conflict;\r\n* focus and activation remain correct;\r\n* reduced state remains readable.\r\n\r\n## Shipâ€™s Log\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* Motion owns row layout;\r\n* GSAP owns ink child;\r\n* no GSAP transform/opacity on `motion.li`;\r\n* insertion and filtering remain stable.\r\n\r\n## Artifact Inspection\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* Motion owns shared shell;\r\n* GSAP owns engraving child;\r\n* focus trap remains correct;\r\n* close returns focus;\r\n* no whole-object GSAP FLIP.\r\n\r\n## Companion Header and Navigation\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* dim targets are explicit;\r\n* no undeclared double `peripheral`;\r\n* navigation remains interactive;\r\n* cleanup restores correct state.\r\n\r\n## Quartermaster\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* command host cannot select controls;\r\n* dialog remains Motion-owned;\r\n* overlay is instance-local;\r\n* receipt reconciliation is stable.\r\n\r\n## Access and Login\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* success final state holds;\r\n* no cleanup snapback;\r\n* failure remains stable;\r\n* route failure is recoverable.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Required PageFlip Tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n1. hidden source exclusion;\r\n2. visible clone qualification;\r\n3. stale clone rejection;\r\n4. current-page target qualification;\r\n5. off-page exclusion where appropriate;\r\n6. no duplicate IDs;\r\n7. correct accessibility tree;\r\n8. manual turn remains StPageFlip-owned;\r\n9. keyboard turn remains StPageFlip-owned;\r\n10. programmatic turn remains StPageFlip-owned;\r\n11. fake GSAP curl scenes are no longer production scenes;\r\n12. content update preserves identity;\r\n13. orientation preserves identity;\r\n14. unmount releases targets and claims.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Required Final-State Tests\r\n\r\nTest access and login:\r\n\r\n1. success response;\r\n2. accepted visual state;\r\n3. delayed route;\r\n4. no snapback;\r\n5. route failure;\r\n6. auth failure;\r\n7. request abort;\r\n8. component unmount;\r\n9. reduced mode;\r\n10. repeated submission.\r\n\r\nPass condition:\r\n\r\n* success never visibly returns to closed or locked before navigation;\r\n* failure never flashes a successful state;\r\n* controls and focus remain correct.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. Mandatory Two-Host Integration Test\r\n\r\nMount:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nHost A\r\nscene: map-reveal\r\npart: route-path\r\n\r\nHost B\r\nscene: route-draw\r\npart: route-path\r\n```\r\n\r\nRun only one scene.\r\n\r\nAssert:\r\n\r\n* only its host changes;\r\n* the second host is untouched;\r\n* metrics report the correct instance;\r\n* cleanup does not affect the other host;\r\n* target counts remain exact;\r\n* no ownership claims leak.\r\n\r\nThis is a release-blocking Claim the Deck test.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 31. Development Showcase Requirements\r\n\r\nUpdate the showcase so it accurately represents the new architecture.\r\n\r\nThe showcase must:\r\n\r\n* register one host per scene;\r\n* show host IDs;\r\n* show instance IDs;\r\n* show target counts;\r\n* show ownership claims;\r\n* simulate conflicts;\r\n* simulate hidden source targets;\r\n* simulate duplicate targets;\r\n* display scene reachability;\r\n* identify fallbacks;\r\n* identify deprecated scenes;\r\n* demonstrate the actual PageFlip runtime.\r\n\r\nThe showcase remains diagnostic.\r\n\r\nA successful showcase scene does not prove production integration.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 32. Accessibility Requirements\r\n\r\nWrapper insertion and host boundaries must preserve accessibility.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* semantic roles remain on the correct node;\r\n* interactive nodes remain interactive;\r\n* decorative GSAP children are hidden from assistive technology;\r\n* PageFlip hidden sources are excluded from the accessibility tree;\r\n* visible page content remains readable;\r\n* focus order remains stable;\r\n* dialog focus trap remains correct;\r\n* focus returns to exact triggers;\r\n* route focus occurs only after navigation;\r\n* ownership fallback remains readable;\r\n* reduced motion remains compatible;\r\n* motion is never the only state signal.\r\n\r\nRun automated accessibility checks and keyboard-focused component or browser tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 33. Final Quality Rule\r\nFIRST_220_BEGIN\r\n# PROJECT LANTERNWAKE\r\n\r\n## Phase 2: Claim the Deck\r\n\r\n### Runtime Ownership, Scene Scoping, and Animation Boundary Enforcement\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 1. Mission\r\n\r\nImplement **Phase 2 of Project Lanternwake**, the complete animation-system rebuilding program for the Forever Treasure / Tall Tale experience.\r\n\r\nThis phase is called:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nClaim the Deck\r\n```\r\n\r\nIts formal engineering scope is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nRuntime Ownership, Scene Scoping, and Animation Boundary Enforcement\r\n```\r\n\r\nPhase 1, **Light the Lantern**, established animation truth:\r\n\r\n* typed target contracts;\r\n* structured presentation outcomes;\r\n* acknowledgment safety;\r\n* replay persistence;\r\n* bounded animation lifecycle behavior;\r\n* reduced-motion authority;\r\n* and explicit scene reachability.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 must now establish **where animations are allowed to operate and which runtime owns every animated property**.\r\n\r\nThe central rule of this phase is:\r\n\r\n> Every animation must operate inside an explicit scene boundary, and every animated property must have exactly one active owner.\r\n\r\nThis phase must make the following failures impossible or explicitly rejected:\r\n\r\n1. A Player event scene querying the entire Player application.\r\n2. A Quartermaster scene targeting ordinary command controls.\r\n3. A GSAP scene selecting both a permanent section object and a temporary event overlay.\r\n4. A hidden PageFlip source element satisfying a visible cinematic requirement.\r\n5. A stale PageFlip clone receiving a current event animation.\r\n6. Motion and GSAP writing transforms to the same DOM node.\r\n7. Motion and GSAP writing opacity to the same DOM node during the same lifecycle.\r\n8. A rejected ownership claim being logged but ignored.\r\n9. DOM order deciding which artifact, marker, route, quest or log entry animates.\r\n10. GSAP cleanup visually undoing a successful login or route transition.\r\n11. Fake GSAP page-flip scenes being treated as real production page-turn implementations.\r\n12. A future animation requirement being lost because it was consolidated into a vague audit row.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 is complete only when animation ownership, scene identity, scene boundaries, PageFlip boundaries and final-state handoffs are enforced by code and tests rather than comments and conventions.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 2. Project Identity and Naming\r\n\r\nUse the following project naming consistently.\r\n\r\n## Program\r\n\r\n```text\r\nProject Lanternwake\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Phase\r\n\r\n```text\r\nPhase 2: Claim the Deck\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Formal scope\r\n\r\n```text\r\nRuntime Ownership, Scene Scoping, and Animation Boundary Enforcement\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Task title\r\n\r\n```text\r\nProject Lanternwake Â· Phase 2: Claim the Deck\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Branch\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncodex/project-lanternwake-phase-2-claim-the-deck\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Phase design record\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Implementation report\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Implementation_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Validation report\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Validation_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Optional migration ledger\r\n\r\nCreate this only if the implementation needs a dedicated per-component migration tracker beyond the governing matrix:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Ownership_Migration_Ledger.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use alternate generic names such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nAnimation_Phase_2_Report.md\r\nownership-fixes.md\r\nanimation-scoping-final.md\r\nphase2-stuff.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nAll Phase 2 artifacts must use the Project Lanternwake naming system.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 3. Governing Documentation\r\n\r\nRead all governing documentation completely before editing source.\r\n\r\nRequired documents:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Full_Audit.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Implementation_Roadmap.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Test_Plan.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/KG_Original_Animation_Audit_Reconciliation_Source.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_Original_Audit_Reconciliation_Ledger.csv\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_1_Design_Record.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso locate and read any completed Phase 1 implementation and validation reports, including likely files such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_1_Implementation_Report.md\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_1_Validation_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf actual filenames differ, locate the authoritative Phase 1 files and record their paths.\r\n\r\nThe original audit baseline was:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n4dbe8c0ae2fbab2785d1d3f26b8d7ba33bf56aee\r\n```\r\n\r\nHowever, implementation must target the **current checked-out repository state**.\r\n\r\nThe audit remains the governing architectural source, but every baseline finding must be revalidated before modifying current code.\r\n\r\nDo not overwrite a newer fix with an older audit assumption.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Phase 1 Dependency Gate\r\n\r\nDo not begin broad Phase 2 component migration until the Phase 1 interfaces have been confirmed.\r\n\r\nVerify that the current implementation includes stable equivalents of:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nSceneTargetContract\r\nSceneTargetRequirement\r\nSceneTargetObservation\r\nScenePreflightReport\r\nPresentationOutcome\r\nPresentationReceipt\r\nScenePlaybackPolicy\r\nSceneAcknowledgmentPolicy\r\nSceneFinalStatePolicy\r\nResolvedMotionPolicy\r\nJournalPhaseOutcome\r\nscene reachability metadata\r\npersisted presentation replay source\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso verify that:\r\n\r\n* required target failure cannot return `presented`;\r\n* duplicate required targets cannot silently pass;\r\n* mandatory presentation failures do not acknowledge events;\r\n* journal phase waits are bounded and abortable;\r\n* browser reduced motion participates in the resolved policy;\r\n* scene reachability is explicit;\r\n* replay is presentation-only.\r\n\r\nIf Phase 1 is incomplete:\r\n\r\n1. identify the precise missing dependency;\r\n2. repair only the minimum blocking Phase 1 contract;\r\n3. document the deviation;\r\n4. do not silently invent a competing replacement architecture.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 must build on Phase 1 rather than bypass it.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Repository Safety and Concurrent Work Protection\r\n\r\nBefore editing anything:\r\n\r\n1. Confirm the repository root.\r\n2. KGâ€™s original external audit additions, identified as `OA-001` through `OA-238`.\r\n\r\nEvery accepted animation requirement must eventually be:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nimplemented\r\nvalidated\r\nsuperseded with documented replacement\r\nor explicitly rejected with approved rationale\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo accepted animation may disappear because:\r\n\r\n* several ideas were consolidated into one matrix row;\r\n* a scene name was too broad;\r\n* architecture work was mistaken for visual implementation;\r\n* a later phase assumed another phase covered it;\r\n* the original audit and Codex audit used different terminology.\r\n\r\n## Phase 2 responsibility\r\n\r\nPhase 2 must:\r\n\r\n1. implement all ownership and scene-scoping requirements assigned to Phase 2;\r\n2. preserve all future animation requirements;\r\n3. map every accepted animation to a roadmap phase;\r\n4. record whether Phase 2 makes that animation architecture-ready;\r\n5. prevent broad implementation rows from hiding specific OA requirements;\r\n6. leave zero accepted requirements with blank phase ownership;\r\n7. leave zero accepted requirements unmapped.\r\n\r\nUse exact statuses:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nnot_started\r\narchitecture_blocked\r\narchitecture_ready\r\npartially_implemented\r\nimplemented\r\nvalidated\r\nblocked\r\nsuperseded\r\nrejected\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not mark a future visual animation `implemented` merely because Phase 2 built its SceneHost or ownership wrapper.\r\n\r\nFor example:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nOA-127 Single new-marker stamp\r\nStatus after Phase 2: architecture_ready\r\n```\r\n\r\nIt becomes `implemented` only when the actual marker stamp visual, trigger, reduced behavior and tests are completed in its assigned later phase.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Required Phase 2 Design Record\r\n\r\nBefore broad implementation, create:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Project_Lanternwake_Phase_2_Design_Record.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe design record must define and freeze:\r\n\r\n1. `SceneHost`\r\n2. `SceneHostKind`\r\n3. `SceneHostRegistration`\r\n4. `SceneHostHandle`\r\n5. `SceneInstanceId`\r\n6. `SceneHostId`\r\n7. `SceneTargetHandle`\r\n8. `ExternalSceneTargetHandle`\r\n9. `OwnershipScope`\r\n10. `AnimationOwnershipClaim`\r\n11. `AnimationOwnershipClaimResult`\r\n12. `AnimatedProperty`\r\n13. `PropertyOwnershipGroup`\r\n14. `SceneFinalStateHandoff`\r\n15. `SceneCleanupPolicy`\r\n16. `PageFlipSourceBoundary`\r\n17. `PageFlipCloneBoundary`\r\n18. scene-host diagnostics;\r\n19. compatibility plan for existing scenes;\r\n20. migration plan into Phase 3 and Phase 4.\r\n\r\nThe design record must explain:\r\n\r\n* why application-root querying is unsafe;\r\n* how host identity differs from scene identity;\r\n* how live playback differs from replay identity;\r\n* how duplicate part names can safely exist in separate hosts;\r\n* how explicit external handles replace broad cross-tree selectors;\r\n* how ownership conflicts are enforced;\r\n* how Motion participates in the ownership system;\r\n* how wrappers separate runtime responsibilities;\r\n* how hidden PageFlip sources are excluded;\r\n* how final-state handoff prevents cleanup snapback;\r\n* how Phase 3 will use the architecture for the persistent Player progression host;\r\n* how Phase 4 will use the same architecture for modern platform ceremonies;\r\n* how future Codex and OA animations remain tracked.\r\n\r\nDo not begin parallel component migrations until the shared architecture interfaces are stable.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Exact Phase Scope\r\n\r\nPhase 2 contains the following mandatory engineering workstreams:\r\n\r\n1. Dedicated SceneHost architecture\r\n2. Unique scene-instance identity\r\n3. Host-local semantic target resolution\r\n4. Explicit external target handles\r\n5. Enforced runtime ownership\r\n6. Motion ownership integration\r\n7. High-risk collision-surface migration\r\n8. StPageFlip source/clone boundary\r\n9. Retirement of fake GSAP page-turn scenes\r\n10. Scene reachability finalization\r\n11. Final-state commit and handoff policies\r\n12. Scene-host diagnostics and telemetry\r\n13. Zero-loss animation tracking\r\n14. Focused tests and browser validation\r\n15. Phase 3 and Phase 4 architecture handoff\r\n\r\nAll workstreams are required.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Explicit Non-Goals\r\n\r\nDo not broadly implement later visual phases during Claim the Deck.\r\n\r\nDo not yet implement:\r\n\r\n* every Player progression ceremony;\r\n* the complete persistent Player progression UI;\r\n* all 238 OA animations;\r\n* all Codex missing-animation rows;\r\n* full platform animation expansion;\r\n* all Player Library animations;\r\n* all Captain Library animations;\r\n* all Studio animations;\r\n* final Rive production artwork;\r\n* final Lottie polish;\r\n* final PageFlip visual tuning;\r\n* complete finale choreography;\r\n* final accessibility tuning;\r\n* final performance polish;\r\n* large decorative ambient systems.\r\n\r\nThose animations remain mandatory in their assigned phases.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 builds the architecture that allows them to be implemented safely.\r\n\r\nA tiny visible fixture or minimal component effect may be added when required to validate ownership or scene scoping.\r\n\r\nDo not convert such a fixture into an unauthorized broad Phase 3â€“6 expansion.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Workstream A: Dedicated SceneHost Architecture\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Required concept\r\n\r\nCreate a reusable SceneHost boundary.\r\n\r\nEvery production cinematic scene must ultimately run inside a dedicated local host rather than querying an entire application role root.\r\n\r\nA SceneHost must provide at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nhost ID\r\nhost kind\r\nscene instance ID\r\nscene name\r\nevent or action ID\r\nroot element\r\ntarget contract\r\nownership scope\r\ncleanup lifetime\r\nresolved motion policy\r\npresentation state\r\n```\r\n\r\nA recommended conceptual API is:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype SceneHostKind =\r\n  | \"gateway\"\r\n  | \"access\"\r\n  | \"player-progression\"\r\n  | \"player-section-enhancement\"\r\n  | \"journal-opening\"\r\n  | \"quartermaster-command\"\r\n  | \"platform-ceremony\"\r\n  | \"development-showcase\";\r\n\r\ntype SceneHostRegistration = {\r\n  hostId: SceneHostId;\r\n  instanceId: SceneInstanceId;\r\n  kind: SceneHostKind;\r\n  sceneName: SceneName;\r\n  eventOrActionId?: string;\r\n  root: HTMLElement;\r\n  targetContract: SceneTargetContract;\r\n  ownershipScope: OwnershipScope;\r\n};\r\n\r\ntype SceneHostHandle = {\r\n  hostId: SceneHostId;\r\n  instanceId: SceneInstanceId;\r\n  root: HTMLElement;\r\n  resolvePart(part: ScenePartName): SceneTargetResolution;\r\n  resolveExternalTarget(\r\n    handle: ExternalSceneTargetHandle\r\n  ): SceneTargetResolution;\r\n  release(): void;\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe exact design may differ, but no required capability may be omitted.\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Query rules\r\n\r\nProduction scenes must not use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ndocument.querySelector\r\ndocument.querySelectorAll\r\nentire Player root queries\r\nentire Quartermaster root queries\r\nunbounded application-shell queries\r\nDOM-order inference\r\n```\r\n\r\nfor scene targets.\r\n\r\nA scene query must:\r\n\r\n1. start from its SceneHost root;\r\n2. remain inside the host;\r\n3. validate scene instance identity;\r\n4. validate target contract;\r\n5. reject stale targets;\r\n6. reject source-tree targets;\r\n7. reject another hostâ€™s targets;\r\n8. reject undeclared external targets.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Required host categories\r\n\r\nImplement support for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngateway\r\naccess\r\nplayer-progression\r\nplayer-section-enhancement\r\njournal-opening\r\nquartermaster-command\r\nplatform-ceremony\r\ndevelopment-showcase\r\n```\r\n\r\nNot every complete visual host must be mounted in Phase 2.\r\n\r\nHowever, the host abstraction must support every category without later phases creating a parallel architecture.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Multiple simultaneous hosts\r\n\r\nThe architecture must support:\r\n\r\n* two hosts with identical part names;\r\n* multiple event hosts;\r\n* live scene and replay scene identities;\r\n* Player and Quartermaster scenes existing simultaneously;\r\n* development showcase instances isolated from production surfaces.\r\n\r\nOne host must never resolve another hostâ€™s target.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Workstream B: Unique Scene-Instance Identity\r\n\r\nEvery active scene invocation must have a unique identity.\r\n\r\nThe identity must distinguish:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nscene name\r\nevent or action\r\nhost\r\nlive versus replay\r\ninvocation sequence\r\n```\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nchapter-release:event-abc123:live:01\r\nchapter-release:event-abc123:replay:02\r\nmap-reveal:event-def456:live:01\r\nquartermaster-login:request-xyz789:live:01\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not rely on:\r\n\r\n* React array indexes;\r\n* timestamps alone;\r\n* scene name alone;\r\n* host name alone;\r\n* current section alone.\r\n\r\nThe instance ID must be available to:\r\n\r\n* the director;\r\n* target resolver;\r\n* ownership registry;\r\n* presentation receipt;\r\n* metrics;\r\n* cleanup;\r\n* interruption handling;\r\n* replay;\r\n* tests;\r\n* development diagnostics.\r\n\r\nStale targets from a previous instance must never satisfy a current scene.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Workstream C: Host-Local Semantic Target Resolution\r\n\r\nRefactor target selection so part names are local to the active host and scene instance.\r\n\r\nThe audit identified repeated scene-part names including:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nroute-path\r\nmap-fog\r\nmap-marker-new\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "BRIEF_LINES_621_1020\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\nartifact-reveal\r\nartifact-light\r\nartifact-slot-target\r\nartifact-connection-path\r\nquest-note-new\r\nquest-stamp\r\nred-thread\r\nlog-entry-new\r\nlog-symbol-new\r\nfinale-ring-outer\r\nfinale-ring-inner\r\nfinale-light-path\r\nworkspace-light\r\nlantern\r\nperipheral\r\n```\r\n\r\nThese part names may continue to exist in different components.\r\n\r\nThey must no longer be globally ambiguous.\r\n\r\n## Required resolution process\r\n\r\nFor each part:\r\n\r\n1. query inside the active SceneHost;\r\n2. verify the scene instance;\r\n3. verify required or optional status;\r\n4. verify cardinality;\r\n5. verify visibility;\r\n6. verify ownership;\r\n7. reject hidden source content;\r\n8. reject stale prior instances;\r\n9. reject unrelated permanent section content;\r\n10. reject another temporary overlay;\r\n11. return a structured resolution receipt.\r\n\r\n## Required failure reasons\r\n\r\nSupport at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntarget-not-found\r\ntarget-outside-host\r\ntarget-hidden\r\ntarget-zero-box\r\ntarget-duplicate\r\ntarget-stale-instance\r\ntarget-source-tree\r\ntarget-wrong-owner\r\ntarget-disconnected\r\ntarget-contract-mismatch\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not turn these into generic `undefined target` warnings.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Workstream D: Explicit External Target Handles\r\n\r\nSome scenes need visual continuity between:\r\n\r\n* global event presentation;\r\n* permanent section content;\r\n* source artifact;\r\n* destination slot;\r\n* route overlay;\r\n* settled map marker;\r\n* shared-layout inspection object.\r\n\r\nDo not solve this with broad selectors.\r\n\r\nCreate explicit target handles.\r\n\r\nRecommended shape:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype ExternalSceneTargetHandle = {\r\n  targetId: string;\r\n  element: HTMLElement;\r\n  owningRuntime: AnimationRuntimeOwner;\r\n  allowedProperties: AnimatedProperty[];\r\n  sourceHostId?: SceneHostId;\r\n  destinationHostId?: SceneHostId;\r\n  lifetime: \"scene\" | \"handoff\";\r\n};\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Required rules\r\n\r\n* source and destination are passed explicitly;\r\n* target identity is validated;\r\n* ownership is validated;\r\n* allowed properties are validated;\r\n* disconnected targets fail;\r\n* stale handles fail;\r\n* cleanup releases handles;\r\n* DOM order never selects source or destination;\r\n* `querySelector()` cannot decide which artifact slot is intended.\r\n\r\nThis is especially important for later:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nartifact award\r\nartifact connection\r\nmarker stamp\r\nroute reveal\r\nship movement\r\nlog insertion\r\nfinale requirement transfer\r\n```\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Workstream E: Enforced Runtime Ownership\r\n\r\nThe current ownership system cannot remain warning-only.\r\n\r\nA rejected ownership claim must prevent the rejected runtime from writing the property.\r\n\r\n## Required tracking\r\n\r\nTrack at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nelement\r\nruntime\r\nproperty or property group\r\nscene instance\r\nhost\r\nclaim ID\r\nclaim start\r\nclaim release\r\nclaim status\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Required runtime owners\r\n\r\nSupport:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngsap\r\nmotion\r\ncss\r\npage-flip\r\nrive\r\nlottie\r\nweb-animations\r\nreact\r\ndnd-kit\r\n```\r\n\r\nWeb Audio does not own DOM properties but may remain represented separately in scene metadata.\r\n\r\n## Required properties\r\n\r\nSupport at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntransform\r\ntranslate\r\nscale\r\nrotate\r\nopacity\r\nlayout\r\nclip-path\r\nfilter\r\nwidth\r\nheight\r\npath-drawing\r\nscroll-position\r\nvisibility\r\n```\r\n\r\nNormalize related properties where required.\r\n\r\nFor example, simultaneous GSAP `x` and Motion layout transforms should conflict under the same transform ownership group.\r\n\r\n## Typed claim result\r\n\r\nUse a typed result such as:\r\n\r\n```ts\r\ntype AnimationOwnershipClaimResult =\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"granted\";\r\n      claimId: string;\r\n    }\r\n  | {\r\n      status: \"rejected\";\r\n      property: AnimatedProperty;\r\n      requestedOwner: AnimationRuntimeOwner;\r\n      existingOwner: AnimationRuntimeOwner;\r\n      existingSceneInstanceId?: SceneInstanceId;\r\n      reason: AnimationOwnershipConflictReason;\r\n    };\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Enforcement behavior\r\n\r\nIf a required GSAP property claim is rejected:\r\n\r\n* do not add the node to the tween;\r\n* record the rejection;\r\n* fail preflight when required;\r\n* use an approved fallback only when the scene contract permits;\r\n* do not mark the scene presented;\r\n* do not acknowledge a mandatory event.\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* warn and continue;\r\n* silently steal ownership;\r\n* clear another runtimeâ€™s active claim;\r\n* weaken target requirements to keep tests green.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Workstream F: Motion Ownership Integration\r\n\r\nMotion must participate in the same architecture.\r\n\r\nA static attribute such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ndata-animation-owner=\"motion\"\r\n```\r\n\r\nis useful for diagnostics but does not enforce ownership.\r\n\r\n## Required wrapper approach\r\n\r\nUse separate nested nodes for different runtime responsibilities.\r\n\r\nExample:\r\n\r\n```tsx\r\n<motion.div data-motion-layout-wrapper>\r\n  <div data-gsap-cinematic-child />\r\n</motion.div>\r\n```\r\n\r\nor:\r\n\r\n```tsx\r\n<div data-gsap-scene-wrapper>\r\n  <motion.div data-motion-interaction-child />\r\n</div>\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Same-node restrictions\r\n\r\nA node may not simultaneously use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion layout or layoutId\r\nand GSAP transform\r\n```\r\n\r\nunless a specific non-overlapping ownership contract proves safety.\r\n\r\nA node may not simultaneously use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion presence opacity\r\nand GSAP opacity\r\n```\r\n\r\nduring the same lifecycle.\r\n\r\nA node may not simultaneously use:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ndnd-kit transform\r\nand Motion or GSAP transform\r\n```\r\n\r\nduring drag ownership.\r\n\r\n## Motion ownership responsibilities\r\n\r\nMotion should continue to own:\r\n\r\n* React presence;\r\n* list layout;\r\n* card layout;\r\n* shared layout;\r\n* dialogs;\r\n* tabs;\r\n* filters;\r\n* section transitions;\r\n* form state transitions;\r\n* ordinary interaction feedback.\r\n\r\nGSAP should continue to own:\r\n\r\n* bounded narrative sequences;\r\n* semantic reveal choreography;\r\n* SVG drawing;\r\n* motion paths;\r\n* server-synchronized ceremonies.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. Workstream G: Confirmed Collision-Surface Migrations\r\n\r\nMigrate each high-risk surface.\r\n\r\n## 16.1 Voyage Chart markers\r\n\r\nCurrent risk:\r\n\r\n* Motion layout and press behavior;\r\n* GSAP marker/map animation;\r\n* same-node transform conflict.\r\n\r\nRequired structure:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion marker layout wrapper\r\n  GSAP marker event-visual child\r\n    stable marker content\r\n```\r\n\r\nOwnership:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion:\r\n- layout\r\n- wrapper transform\r\n- press interaction\r\n\r\nGSAP:\r\n- inner stamp\r\n- pulse\r\n- bounded reveal\r\n\r\nCSS:\r\n- material and static states\r\n```\r\n\r\nVerify:\r\n\r\n* marker insertion;\r\n* marker update;\r\n* marker focus;\r\n* marker click;\r\n* marker stamp;\r\n* section transition;\r\n* reduced motion;\r\n* duplicate host isolation.\r\n\r\n## 16.2 Shipâ€™s Log entries\r\n\r\nRequired structure:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion list-row wrapper\r\n  semantic log content\r\n  GSAP fresh-ink child\r\n  GSAP symbol child\r\n```\r\n\r\nOwnership:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion:\r\n- row presence\r\n- list layout\r\n- row position\r\n\r\nGSAP:\r\n- new ink\r\n- new symbol\r\n- bounded detail reveal\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe `motion.li` must not receive GSAP transform or opacity writes.\r\n\r\n## 16.3 Artifact Inspection\r\n\r\nRequired structure:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion shared-layout artifact shell\r\n  stable artifact image/object\r\n  GSAP engraving child\r\n  GSAP detail-light child\r\n```\r\n\r\nOwnership:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion:\r\n- shared-layout open and close\r\n- dialog presence\r\n\r\nGSAP:\r\n- engraving stroke\r\n- inner illumination\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "BRIEF_LINES_1021_1420\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 1.3 seconds\nOutput:\n- bounded detail reveal\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not use GSAP FLIP on the Motion `layoutId` node.\r\n\r\nFocus must:\r\n\r\n* enter the dialog;\r\n* remain trapped;\r\n* return to the exact source artifact.\r\n\r\n## 16.4 Companion Header and Navigation\r\n\r\nCurrent problem:\r\n\r\n* generic `peripheral` names;\r\n* multiple visible matches;\r\n* undeclared dimming cardinality.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n* explicitly named dimming surfaces;\r\n* deliberate cardinality;\r\n* Motion-owned header/navigation layout;\r\n* GSAP-owned dedicated cinematic overlay or child;\r\n* no global scene taking ownership of navigation interaction nodes.\r\n\r\n## 16.5 Quartermaster\r\n\r\nRequired structure:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nQuartermaster shell\r\n  Motion command controls\r\n  Motion confirmation dialog\r\n  dedicated GSAP command SceneHost\r\n  Motion result and receipt layer\r\n```\r\n\r\nGSAP must not target:\r\n\r\n* ordinary action buttons;\r\n* form controls;\r\n* recent event rows;\r\n* unrelated command cards.\r\n\r\nEach command overlay must be local to its scene instance.\r\n\r\n## 16.6 Access and login\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nMotion:\r\n- form presence\r\n- pending state\r\n- validation errors\r\n- permission state\r\n\r\nGSAP:\r\n- bounded accepted/rejected cinematic object\r\n- route-transition ceremony\r\n\r\nRive:\r\n- future seal/lock internal state\r\n```\r\n\r\nSuccess must hold its final pose until navigation.\r\n\r\nFailure must return to a stable interactive state.\r\n\r\nNo visible snapback may occur before route commit.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. Workstream H: StPageFlip Source and Clone Boundary\r\n\r\nStPageFlip remains the sole owner of physical page curl.\r\n\r\nThe hidden React source tree must not qualify as live cinematic content.\r\n\r\n## Hidden source requirements\r\n\r\nHidden source nodes must:\r\n\r\n* not satisfy visible scene requirements;\r\n* not receive current scene-instance identity accidentally;\r\n* not remain focusable;\r\n* not remain in the accessibility tree when duplicated by visible content;\r\n* not be selected by GSAP;\r\n* not satisfy cardinality;\r\n* not retain conflicting duplicate IDs;\r\n* not remain owned after update or unmount.\r\n\r\n## Visible clone requirements\r\n\r\nVisible clones must:\r\n\r\n* be tied to a PageFlip instance;\r\n* be tied to page identity;\r\n* be explicitly marked as visible PageFlip content;\r\n* expose only deliberate cinematic targets;\r\n* reject stale clone instances;\r\n* provide lifecycle labels.\r\n\r\n## Attribute strategy\r\n\r\nDetermine and document whether the implementation will:\r\n\r\n* strip cinematic attributes from hidden sources;\r\n* namespace attributes in clones;\r\n* rebind explicit target handles after PageFlip initialization;\r\n* exclude sources through SceneHost boundaries;\r\n* or use a combination.\r\n\r\nDo not blindly remove every `data-*` attribute.\r\n\r\nPreserve attributes required for:\r\n\r\n* PageFlip;\r\n* accessibility;\r\n* content identity;\r\n* testing.\r\n\r\n## Required target fixture\r\n\r\nCreate a fixture containing:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nhidden React source\r\nvisible PageFlip clone\r\nstale prior clone\r\npermanent section target\r\ntemporary event target\r\n```\r\n\r\nA scene must select only the declared visible target.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. Workstream I: Retire Fake GSAP Page-Turn Scenes\r\n\r\nRetire:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nmanual-page-flip\r\nprogrammatic-page-flip\r\n```\r\n\r\nThese are redundant with StPageFlip.\r\n\r\nRequired actions:\r\n\r\n* remove or deprecate registry entries;\r\n* remove production reachability;\r\n* update callers;\r\n* update tests;\r\n* update showcase labels;\r\n* update audit matrix;\r\n* update reconciliation ledger;\r\n* document replacement with StPageFlip lifecycle labels.\r\n\r\nStPageFlip must own:\r\n\r\n* pointer turns;\r\n* drag turns;\r\n* keyboard turns;\r\n* programmatic turns;\r\n* page index;\r\n* page orientation;\r\n* physical curl;\r\n* turn lifecycle.\r\n\r\nGSAP may run:\r\n\r\n* before-turn narrative effects;\r\n* after-commit effects;\r\n* after-settle effects.\r\n\r\nGSAP may not imitate the curl.\r\n\r\nThe development showcase should demonstrate the real PageFlip runtime rather than a fake GSAP timeline placeholder.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Workstream J: Remaining Scene Dispositions\r\n\r\nRevalidate:\r\n\r\n```text\r\njournal-open\r\nchapter-heading\r\nprose-ink\r\nmarker-stamp\r\nship-course\r\nartifact-inspection\r\n```\r\n\r\nExpected direction:\r\n\r\n## `journal-open`\r\n\r\nMerge into or deprecate in favor of the real journal opening state machine.\r\n\r\nDo not retain two authoritative journal-opening systems.\r\n\r\n## `chapter-heading`\r\n\r\nRetain as an internal reusable sub-sequence or future contract if needed.\r\n\r\nDo not claim production reachability without a real trigger.\r\n\r\n## `prose-ink`\r\n\r\nRetain only if it will target a visible scoped page host and has a later semantic trigger.\r\n\r\n## `marker-stamp`\r\n\r\nRetain as a future contract for a real marker-specific trigger after scoped chart architecture exists.\r\n\r\n## `ship-course`\r\n\r\nRetain as a future contract for authoritative position movement with explicit path and ship handles.\r\n\r\n## `artifact-inspection`\r\n\r\nDo not connect as a whole-object GSAP scene.\r\n\r\nMotion owns shared layout.\r\n\r\nRetain only an inner engraving/detail sub-sequence if useful.\r\n\r\nFor each scene record:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nproduction\r\nlegacy\r\ndevelopment-only\r\nfuture-contract\r\nmerged\r\ndeprecated\r\n```\r\n\r\nNo ambiguous status may remain.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Workstream K: Final-State Commit and Handoff\r\n\r\nImplement explicit final-state behavior.\r\n\r\nSupport at least:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nrevert-immediately\r\nhold-final-until-unmount\r\ncommit-final-state\r\nreconcile-then-revert\r\nfallback-to-static-state\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Policy meaning\r\n\r\n### Revert immediately\r\n\r\nUse only for non-semantic decorative work.\r\n\r\n### Hold final until unmount\r\n\r\nUse for route-changing success scenes where the final state must remain visible until navigation.\r\n\r\n### Commit final state\r\n\r\nUse when the sceneâ€™s end state becomes the current visual truth.\r\n\r\n### Reconcile then revert\r\n\r\nUse when a temporary overlay hands settled state to a permanent component.\r\n\r\n### Fallback to static state\r\n\r\nUse when the runtime cannot complete but a readable approved state can be rendered.\r\n\r\n## Required receipt fields\r\n\r\nRecord:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nfinalStatePolicy\r\nfinalStateCommitted\r\nhandoffTargetId\r\nhandoffStarted\r\nhandoffCompleted\r\nhandoffFailure\r\ncleanupStarted\r\ncleanupCompleted\r\ncleanupResult\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Access/login gate\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* success response;\r\n* success animation;\r\n* delayed route commit;\r\n* no snapback;\r\n* route failure;\r\n* authentication failure;\r\n* abort;\r\n* unmount;\r\n* reduced mode;\r\n* repeated submission.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Workstream L: Diagnostics and Telemetry\r\n\r\nDevelopment and test diagnostics must expose:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nProject Lanternwake phase\r\nscene name\r\nscene instance ID\r\nhost ID\r\nhost kind\r\nevent or action ID\r\nruntime\r\ntarget part\r\nrequired or optional\r\ntarget identity\r\nvisibility result\r\nduplicate result\r\nownership property\r\nownership result\r\nfinal-state policy\r\nhandoff result\r\ncleanup result\r\n```\r\n\r\nSupport clear failure codes:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ntarget-not-found\r\ntarget-outside-host\r\ntarget-hidden\r\ntarget-zero-box\r\ntarget-duplicate\r\ntarget-stale-instance\r\ntarget-source-tree\r\ntarget-disconnected\r\nownership-rejected\r\nhandoff-failed\r\ncleanup-failed\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not log:\r\n\r\n* invitation codes;\r\n* PIN values;\r\n* passwords;\r\n* secret story content;\r\n* private event payloads;\r\n* unpublished Creator content;\r\n* authentication tokens.\r\n\r\nProduction users should receive safe readable fallback behavior, not internal diagnostics.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. Workstream M: Zero-Loss Requirement Tracking\r\n\r\nUpdate the controlling requirement artifacts.\r\n\r\nRequired:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_Original_Audit_Reconciliation_Ledger.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nAdd these columns if they do not exist:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nImplementation Status\r\nRoadmap Phase\r\nProject Lanternwake Phase\r\nArchitecture Dependency\r\nScene Host Required\r\nOwnership Contract Required\r\nTarget Contract Required\r\nBlocked By\r\nImplemented In Commit\r\nValidation Status\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Phase 2 architecture rows\r\n\r\nWhen complete:\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "BRIEF_LINES_1421_1656\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 1 seconds\nOutput:\n```text\r\nimplemented\r\n```\r\n\r\nor:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvalidated\r\n```\r\n\r\n## Future visual rows enabled by Phase 2\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\n```text\r\narchitecture_ready\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not mark them implemented.\r\n\r\n## Programmatic validation\r\n\r\nVerify:\r\n\r\n* every accepted `OA-*` item has a phase;\r\n* every accepted Codex matrix item has a phase;\r\n* every accepted item has a correct library;\r\n* every accepted item has a trigger;\r\n* every accepted item has replay policy;\r\n* every accepted item has reduced-motion behavior;\r\n* every accepted item has acceptance criteria;\r\n* every accepted item remains mapped;\r\n* every Phase 2 item has evidence or a blocker.\r\n\r\nThe final unmapped accepted requirement count must equal:\r\n\r\n```text\r\n0\r\n```\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. | tests, browser, database copy, ports 3100/3200, full gate                                                   | I1         | exact exit statuses and isolation proof            |\n| F1 finalization |\n\nExactly one agent writes a file at a time. `PlayerExperience.tsx` is never split across replay and acknowledgment lanes. `src/app/layout.tsx`, `src/styles/tokens.css`, the existing shell/platform/landing/Studio/Tall Tale changes, Prisma schemas, manifests/lockfiles, generated files, port 3000, and canonical local database are forbidden unless the coordinator explicitly records a new handoff.\n\n## 18. Shared resources and runtime strategy\n\n| Resource                      | Owner                      | Rule                                                                                                  |\n| ----------------------------- | -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| Port 3000 / normal dev server | none during implementation | Inspect only; do not stop an unknown owner.                                                           |\n| Playwright port 3100          | final validation owner     | One isolated server and database only.                                                                |\n| Production proof port 3200    | final validation owner     | Used once through repository validation.                                                              |\n| Browser                       | final validation owner     | One stateful mutation controller; read-only shards only when isolated.                                |\n| Database/seed                 | final validation owner     | Unique copied SQLite database, absolute URL, nonce proof, canonical hash/size/mtime before and after. |\n| Prisma                        | no writer planned          | Existing records are sufficient; no migration.                                                        |\n| Manifests/lockfile            | no writer planned          | No new dependency is required.                                                                        |\n| Full build/E2E/validate       | final validation owner     | Deduplicated and serialized.                                                                          |\n| Chat/docs synchronization     | coordinator                | Run once after all workers finish.                                                                    |\n\nDirect `npm run test:e2e` is not accepted as safe mutation evidence until the request's database identity proof is implemented. A worktree or alternate port alone is insufficient.\n\n## 19. Testing strategy\n\n| Layer            | Focus                                                                         | Parallelism                                  | Required treatment                         |\n| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |\n| Type/lint/format | frozen interfaces and integrated source                                       | focused during lanes; full once              | failures classified, never hidden          |\n| Unit/component   | target truth, receipts, Journal, motion, replay, acknowledgment, reachability | independent files may run concurrently       | exact pass/fail/skipped counts             |\n| Assets           | existing contracts only                                                       | serialized with final checks                 | no false production Rive claim             |\n| API/data         | sanitizer, latest replay source, viewed validation                            | focused, no live DB mutation unless isolated | privacy sentinels and campaign/type checks |\n| Browser          | selected Phase 1 flows only                                                   | one mutation owner                           | compliant database proof first             |\n| Build            | integrated production build                                                   | once                                         | exact exit status                          |\n| Full repository  | `npm run validate`                                                            | once                                         | integrated state only                      |\n\nPhase 1 does not claim the complete 102 event/section matrix. It does require focused proof for successful and missing-target chapter release, failed acknowledgment, approved fallback, refresh replay, zero-mutation replay, browser reduced motion, and safe affected access transitions when the isolated runtime is available.\n\n## 20. Assumptions and unknowns\n\n| ID  | Assumption or unknown                                                       | Impact                                                    | Verification                                                                                              | Status                                                                         |\n| --- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| A1  | Current 28-scene registry remains unchanged during implementation.          | Contract count/callers change.                            | Re-scan registry before integration.                                                                      | Verified at preflight.                                                         |\n| A2  | Existing `ProgressEvent` plus public snapshot is sufficient for replay.     | A migration might be needed.                              | Unit projection and historical sparse-payload fallback tests.                                             | Supported; no migration planned.                                               |\n| A3  | Current broad roots can satisfy every production target contract.           | Some scenes will truthfully fall back/fail until Phase 2. | Focused DOM and browser fixtures.                                                                         | Compatibility roots remain deliberate; truthful fallback/failure is preserved. |\n| A4  | Concurrent shell work remains out of Phase 1 files.                         | File ownership collision.                                 | Recheck status before each write handoff.                                                                 | Active risk.                                                                   |\n| A5  | Mutation E2E requires explicit database identity and canonical-state proof. | Unsafe mutation E2E must fail closed.                     | Isolated copy, nonce identity route, absolute URL, mutation observation, canonical hash/size/mtime proof. | Implemented in the validation harness; final full rerun pending.               |\n\n## 21. Risk register\n\n| ID  | Risk                                                               | Probability | Impact   | Mitigation / recovery                                                            | Owner                    | Status                                                          |\n| --- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| K1  | Concurrent shell work overwritten or staged.                       | Medium      | High     | Path ownership, recheck hashes/status, path-scoped diffs/staging only.           | Coordinator              | Open                                                            |\n| K2  | Strict targets expose current broad-host failures.                 | High        | High     | Truthful typed failure/readable fallback; defer dedicated hosts to Phase 2.      | Architecture/Player      | Mitigated by typed outcomes; Phase 2 host work remains.         |\n| K3  | Player acknowledgment still leaks through chapter `ViewedContent`. | Medium      | High     | Gate both ceremony and content-view paths while mandatory attempt is unresolved. | Player                   | Implemented and focused-tested; final browser gate pending.     |\n| K4  | Replay projection exposes private payload.                         | Medium      | High     | Allowlisted typed composition; sentinel tests; never return raw rows.            | Replay/security          | Implemented and focused-tested.                                 |\n| K5  | Journal promises/timers survive abort.                             | High        | High     | Idempotent race/cleanup; under-100-ms tests.                                     | Journal                  | Unit proof green; final browser cleanup gate pending.           |\n| K6  | Motion provider conflicts with concurrent layout/tokens.           | Medium      | Medium   | Publish root attribute inside provider; avoid concurrent files.                  | Motion/coordinator       | Resolved through provider-owned root attribute.                 |\n| K7  | E2E touches canonical database.                                    | Medium      | Critical | Fail-closed unique copy/absolute URL/nonce/hash proof; no mutation otherwise.    | Validation               | Harness implemented; final canonical-state proof pending rerun. |\n| K8  | Operation result migration breaks GM callers.                      | Medium      | High     | Generic receipt retains `operationResult`; final full run pending.              |\n| K10 | Documentation marks future phases complete.                        | Low         | High     | Update only implemented Phase 1 rows and limitations.                            | Coordinator              | Open                                                            |\n\n## 22. Decision log\n\n| ID  | Decision                                                                            | Evidence / reason                                                                                                                                                            |\n| --- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| D1  | No Prisma migration for Phase 1 replay.                                             | Existing immutable `ProgressEvent` identity plus authorized public snapshot is sufficient.                                                                                   |\n| D2  | Use a generic `PresentationReceipt<T>` with `operationResult`.                      | Preserves current operation callers while separating presentation truth.                                                                                                     |\n| D3  | Provider owns resolved motion; no root-layout edit initially.                       | Eliminates per-consumer authority while preserving concurrent shell work.                                                                                                    |\n| D4  | Three deprecated and five future contracts among the eight unreachable scenes.      | Current caller and runtime ownership revalidation.                                                                                                                           |\n| D5  | Snapshot carries latest replayable chapter presentation.                            | Existing authenticated, private, read-only route; avoids a raw-event endpoint.                                                                                               |\n| D6  | Both ceremony and content viewed mutations are gated.                               | Snapshot refresh currently can mark chapter content viewed independently.                                                                                                    |\n| D7  | Phase 1 keeps compatibility roots and documents limitations.                        | Full persistent/global host is an explicit non-goal.                                                                                                                         |\n| D8  | Approved fallback is an explicit verified callback, not registry metadata alone.    | The Director needs evidence that a readable semantic state actually completed before returning `presented-fallback`.                                                         |\n| D9  | Structured presentation telemetry is a bounded projection, not serialized receipts. | Default 100 / maximum 250 events and 64 target / 32 label caps prevent unbounded retention while allowlists and redaction preserve privacy.                                  |\n| D10 | `html[data-motion-level]` is the single CSS motion authority.                       | One provider-resolved policy prevents browser, product, runtime, and stylesheet motion decisions from diverging.                                                             |\n| D11 | Validation must prove runtime and database ownership before mutation E2E.           | A unique copied database, nonce-backed identity route, owned port/process tree, mutation observation, and canonical before/after fingerprint make the safety claim testable. |\n\n## 23. Discovery log\n\n| ID  | Finding                                                                                        | Plan effect                                                                                                   |\n| --- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| X1  | Source is unchanged from audit baseline; only audit documents were added in committed history. | Audit defects remain valid but current code is revalidated.                                                   |\n| X2  | Worktree contains divergent unified-shell work.                                                | Preserve 36 pre-existing paths; avoid layout/tokens collision.                                                |\n| X3  | Reconciliation ledger is absent; source has 238 complete OA items.                             | Preserve requirements; do not perform broad reconciliation.                                                   |\n| X4  | Current showcase omits two registered scenes and labels no reachability.                       | Add reachability truth without treating showcase as production proof.                                         |\n| X5  | Viewed endpoint does not validate event ownership/type/release eligibility.                    | Include defense-in-depth route hardening.                                                                     |\n| X6  | Baseline mutation E2E configuration lacked requested DB identity proof.                        | A fail-closed isolated runtime/database validation wrapper was implemented; final full proof remains pending. |\n\n## 24. Failure recovery\n\n- A failed implementation lane returns its partial diff, tests, blocker, and ownership release; independent lanes continue.\n- Interface conflict returns to the architecture owner before consumers are changed.\n- Unexpected overlap with concurrent work stops the affected writer; the coordinator compares both changes and preserves both.\n- A failed focused test is classified as task regression, pre-existing, environmental, intermittent, or unresolved; evidence is retained.\n- Database identity mismatch immediately stops mutation testing. Canonical state is rechecked; no assumption of safety is permitted.\n- No failed lane triggers reset, clean, stash-all, or restart of completed work.\n\n## 25. Completion gate\n\nPhase 1 is complete only when all 28 scenes carry truthful target/reachability/policy contracts; director receipts cannot report `presented` for missing, hidden, duplicate, off-host, stale, or ownership-rejected required targets; mandatory chapter release acknowledgment is receipt-gated and retryable; replay survives refresh with immutable identity and no mutation; Journal waits are bounded and abortable; one provider-resolved motion policy reaches every Phase 1 runtime interface; deprecated/future scenes are not reported as production complete; focused and integrated validation are complete or explicitly blocked with evidence; concurrent work is preserved; governing documents reflect implemented truth; and repository chat/development-document synchronization has passed.\n\nCurrent outcome: implementation and the 46-file / 297-test integrated static gate are complete. Final full validation, evidence reconciliation, development-document synchronization, and chat synchronization are still pending; therefore this record does not yet declare Phase 1 complete.\n\nPhase 1 stops after review. ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:26:02.959Z

Two early findings materially affect the critical path:

- **R1:** Phase 1’s named contracts exist, but its own record says closure is pending. More importantly, the journal wait can stall before its timeout begins when `requestAnimationFrame` is suspended; that violates Phase 1’s bounded-wait prerequisite.
- **R2:** Phase 1 preflight still passes a broad root into scene builders, which then re-query descendants. There is no SceneHost registry or canonical resolved-target set, so nested hosts can still cross-select—the central Phase 2 defect is confirmed.

I’m verifying the two-file Phase 1 repair boundary now. If unchanged, I’ll use the final worker slot for that minimal prerequisite fix while the SceneHost design finishes.

## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T21:26:53.049Z

R3 independently confirms the ownership problem is deeper than diagnostics: the registry keys exact strings rather than property groups, has no host/instance scope or stale sweep, and runtime wrappers do not acquire live leases. Because builders re-query after preflight, even a rejected optional node can still reach GSAP. That aligns with R2’s canonical-target gap, so the shared design must freeze both contracts together before any component writer starts.

R10 confirms the repository’s full validation wrapper has a real fail-closed copied-database/nonce/PID proof, but raw `npm run test:e2e` is not safe for mutation evidence because it can inherit the normal repository environment. I will not run direct mutating E2E; the coordinator-owned final browser gate will go through the isolation wrapper, and the testing documentation will be corrected accordingly.

(attribute as AnimationRuntimeOwner)\r\n   54:     : null;\r\n   55: }\r\n   56: \r\n   57: function updateOwnerAttribute(element: Element, state: OwnershipState) {\r\n   58:   // A component-authored ownership declaration is source truth. Claims made by\r\n   59:   // this registry must neither replace it while active nor remove it on release.\r\n   60:   if (state.declarativeAttribute !== null) return;\r\n   61:   if (state.claims.length) element.setAttribute(\"data-animation-owner\", state.claims.at(-1)!.owner);\r\n   62:   else element.removeAttribute(\"data-animation-owner\");\r\n   63: }\r\n   64: \r\n   65: function releaseClaim(element: Element, token: symbol) {\r\n   66:   const state = registry.get(element);\r\n   67:   if (!state) return false;\r\n   68:   const remaining = state.claims.filter((claim) => claim.token !== token);\r\n   69:   const released = remaining.length !== state.claims.length;\r\n   70:   const nextState = { ...state, claims: remaining };\r\n   71:   if (remaining.length) registry.set(element, nextState);\r\n   72:   else registry.delete(element);\r\n   73:   updateOwnerAttribute(element, nextState);\r\n   74:   return released;\r\n   75: }\r\n   76: \r\n   77: function sanitizedOwnershipWarning(evidence: AnimationOwnershipEvidence) {\r\n   78:   const part = evidence.part ? ` part=${evidence.part}` : \"\";\r\n   79:   return `[animation ownership] owner=${evidence.owner}${part} rejectedOwner=${evidence.rejectedOwner ?? \"unknown\"} properties=${evidence.conflictingProperties.join(\",\")}`;\r\n   80: }\r\n   81: \r\n   82: export function claimAnimationOwnershipWithEvidence(\r\n   83:   element: Element,\r\n   84:   owner: AnimationOwner,\r\n   85:   properties: readonly string[],\r\n   86:   part?: string,\r\n   87: ): AnimationOwnershipLease {\r\n   88:   const normalizedOwner = normalizeAnimationRuntimeOwner(owner);\r\n   89:   const normalizedProperties = [...new Set(properties)];\r\n   90:   const existingState = registry.get(element);\r\n   91:   const declarativeAttribute = existingState\r\n   92:     ? existingState.declarativeAttribute\r\n   93:     : element.getAttribute(\"data-animation-owner\");\r\n   94:   const declarativeOwner = existingState ? existingState.declarativeOwner : declarativeOwnerFrom(declarativeAttribute);\r\n   95:   const declarativeConflict =\r\n   96:     declarativeAttribute !== null && (declarativeOwner === null || declarativeOwner !== normalizedOwner);\r\n   97:   const claims = existingState?.claims ??
